description: Evaluates enterprise asset management maturity—endpoint, network, and application asset tracking, consolidated policies, inventory and lifecycle processes, threat protection, supply chain visibility, shadow IT, custodianship, and SOC logging.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An enterprise asset management service exists, is scoped across endpoint, network, and application assets, and is reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Endpoint, network, and application asset scope

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Enterprise asset management KPIs and KRIs—including inventory accuracy, provisioning velocity, and shadow asset discovery—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly across asset types.
evaluation_focus: Documented KPIs and KRIs across asset types
Inventory accuracy and lifecycle velocity metrics

capabilities.name: Enterprise asset management system
capabilities.description: A centralized enterprise asset management system automates lifecycle functions, is reviewed at least quarterly, and integrates with access, logging, and compliance systems.
evaluation_focus: Integration with access, logging, and compliance systems

capabilities.name: SSRM and procurement policy
capabilities.description: SSRM strategy and procurement policies govern third-party and managed assets across endpoint, application, and network domains and are reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Unmanaged and mobile assets
capabilities.description: BYOD and mobile asset policies govern unmanaged and mobile device provisioning, security, network access, and self-registration enterprise-wide.

capabilities.name: Managed asset security policies
capabilities.description: Formalized security policies for endpoint, application, and network managed assets apply enterprise-wide and are reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Managed asset provisioning and deprovisioning policies
capabilities.description: Formalized provisioning and deprovisioning policies for endpoint, application, and network managed assets apply enterprise-wide and are reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Asset inventory and registration lifecycle
capabilities.description: Inventory tracking and registration or de-registration processes cover endpoint, application, and network assets with automation and regular review.

capabilities.name: Self-service provisioning, deprovisioning, and migration
capabilities.description: Self-service provisioning and deprovisioning plus migration processes cover endpoint, application, and network assets with automation where appropriate.

capabilities.name: Threat protection and supply chain visibility
capabilities.description: Threat protections and supply chain visibility span endpoint, application, and network assets with enterprise-wide coverage.

capabilities.name: Asset access and user management
capabilities.description: Identity and context-based access control governs asset platform users with regular review.
evaluation_focus: Identity and context-based asset access control

capabilities.name: Asset management custodians
capabilities.description: Separate asset management custodians are assigned by asset type with clear separation of duties.
evaluation_focus: Separate asset management custodians by asset type

capabilities.name: Shadow asset handling
capabilities.description: Shadow IT is managed enterprise-wide with automated discovery where possible.

capabilities.name: Enterprise asset management logging
capabilities.description: Asset lifecycle logs and dynamic state changes are logged securely to the SOC.
evaluation_focus: Documented asset lifecycle logging to SOC
Dynamic asset state changes to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
